Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10653/

Patch Set 1: Fails

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/10653/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, approved

James, thanks for the patches.  Looks like the build failed on SLES11 SP2 due to:

 osc_quota.c:68: error: 'NO_QUOTA'

this was caused by http://review.whamcloud.com/3689, which has since been reverted, but your patch needs to be rebased.

Note that our goal for kernels beyond Linux 3.0 is to allow completely patchless build and test.  The one remaining patch that prevents this today is dev_read_only, which is needed for Lustre failover and recovery testing, as described in LU-20.

Do you have any cycles to look at dm-flakey to figure out if it is possible to enable the "drop_writes" feature on an existing device (i.e. in the middle of using a device when an OBD_FAIL_LOC or lctl command issuing ioctl(OBD_IOC_SET_READONLY)?

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

James, please don't set "+1" on your own patches.  It is assumed that you already approve of your own work, but this otherwise makes it harder to see if the patch has been inspected properly.

Of course, setting "-1" is OK for your own patch if you find some problem after it was submitted.

Patch Set 9: (4 inline comments)

I'll only comment on the SLES issues but I expect they apply just as much to the fc18 patches.

We shouldn't need a separate kernel to support another filesystem. I understand the realities of supporting existing kernels and there's not a lot to be done about that in a module.

But for things like the block queue defaults -- these are adjustable at runtime. There just needs to be an API that acts as a max(hw_val,api_val) before actually accepting it. The drivers already have this knowledge. The patch should take the form of something that might be accepted as an API, even if it may not be because there are no internal kernel users. Since several distributions modify the value, I think there's a decent case to be made.

Patch Set 9:

Toro Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 9: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.3/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/78592ade-67bb-11e2-9eee-52540035b04c).  Ran 12 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-quota.

Patch Set 9:

Jeff work has been in progress for some time to do a patch less kernel, see LU-20. It does make more sense to make the tuneables as module parameters. As for the other patches we have the dev_read_only patch which can in theory be replaced by dm-flaky, see LU-684.bh_lru_size_config.patch is a optimzation patch for Lustre SMP scaling so if you don't include it then you lose some performance gains. Yes raid5-mmp-unplug-dev-sles11sp2.patch is a actually fix that is needed. Have to talk to Andreas about the status of these patches going upstream. I plan to move this the LU-1812 jira ticket.

parents.subject: LU-2677 utils: fix swabbing of f_oid

commit.subject: LU-1812 kernel: 3.7/FC18 server patches
commit.message:
LU-1812 kernel: 3.7/FC18 server patches

This patch extends lustre server support to FC18 running a
3.7.2-201 kernel with a ZFS backend. At this time ldiskfs
is not supported on FC18. This patch provides block level
performance optimizations and the ability to simulate fail
over for ldiskfs when it is provided. The jbd2-jcberr patch
was not ported to these new platforms for reasons described
in LU-433.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I2a86d7ca4e068686372eb74f85b1443d24e245c4
